upcomingEvents.title: Upcoming Events
upcomingEvents.body: The current Vegas event stack holds 4 events inside 14 days and 6 inside 30 days, out of 15 active future event rows. EDC's final festival day is May 17 and EDC Week runs through May 19, so the primary outreach window is now, not next week.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Map short-window event compression into immediate sales and service timing priorities.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: When a meaningful share of the future event book is compressed into the next two weeks, Kevin's edge comes from sequence and timing rather than broader prospecting.
quantResearchProtocol: Compare those counts with the full active future-event inventory.
Identify the named near-term anchor events.
Translate the concentration into outreach urgency and service sequencing.
inferenceConstraints: Do not treat the calendar as evenly distributed when the near-term window is compressed.
Do not separate event concentration from outreach timing.
Do not issue urgency without explicit count math.
outputRequirements: Name the current anchor event window.
Provide a concrete outreach timing instruction.

sourceRecords.recordHint: future event rows used for 14-day and 30-day counts
EDC Week and EDC festival timing reference

notes: Upcoming-events math is based on live vegas.events counts plus the public EDC timing reference.
Operational focus is the immediate outreach window.

aiSalesStrategy.title: AI Sales Strategy
aiSalesStrategy.body: All 10 currently scored accounts clear the >=65 priority threshold, and the average account score is 69.68 with mean event-impact score 33.29. That means Kevin should sequence accounts by event adjacency and impact first, not waste time broadening the list, while using today's energy-led cost backdrop as a cost-certainty pitch enhancer.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Convert account score saturation and event timing into a usable outreach order for Kevin.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: When all tracked accounts qualify as high priority, the problem shifts from filtering to sequencing by demand timing and conversion probability.
quantResearchProtocol: Count high-priority accounts and compare that count with total scored accounts.
Use average score and event-impact metrics to characterize list quality.
Prioritize by event adjacency first and impact second.
Use energy-led market pressure only to sharpen pitch urgency, not to replace account ordering.
inferenceConstraints: Do not dilute focus by treating all accounts as equally urgent.
Do not detach the sales sequence from service readiness and event timing.
Do not hide the threshold logic behind generic AI language.
outputRequirements: Quote the average score and event-impact math.

sourceRecords.recordHint: latest scored-account window and >=65 threshold count
energy-led buyer backdrop used for sales-urgency framing

notes: Sales sequencing is threshold-driven and event-aware.
Energy context sharpens timing language but does not replace account ordering.

restaurantAccounts.title: Restaurant Accounts
restaurantAccounts.body: There are 10 active tracked accounts, 10 scored accounts in the current window, and 10 accounts above the high-priority threshold. That is a 100.0% qualified-rate on the current scored set, so the list is saturated and must be sequenced, not filtered.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Identify whether the current account book is selective or saturated, then set the right field-execution focus.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: A saturated high-priority book means field performance depends on sequencing discipline, not on discovering more targets.
quantResearchProtocol: Compare active-account count, scored-account count, and high-priority count.
Compute the qualification rate on the scored set.
Classify the book as selective or saturated.
Translate the classification into the next operational focus.
inferenceConstraints: Do not pretend the list is selective when qualification rate is 100%.
Do not use one-dimensional priority talk once the opportunity set is saturated.
Do not omit the need for sequencing discipline.
outputRequirements: State active, scored, and high-priority counts.
Explain whether the book is selective or saturated.

notes: Opportunity-set interpretation is count-based and explicit.
Field focus shifts to sequencing when the book is saturated.

fryerTracking.title: Fryer Equipment Tracking
fryerTracking.body: Fryer coverage exists for all 10 tracked sites, but populated fryer-count telemetry is still 0 of 10. Operationally that means service planning knows who is in scope before the EDC window, but it still cannot rank equipment intensity with confidence until direct fryer-count sync lands.

strategicSpecialInstructions.strategicObjective: Separate account-coverage completeness from equipment-telemetry completeness so service teams do not overstate readiness.
strategicSpecialInstructions.neuralConnectionThesis: A fully covered account roster with zero populated fryer counts creates false completeness unless field teams explicitly treat telemetry precision as missing.
quantResearchProtocol: Count fryer rows and populated fryer_count values separately.
Measure telemetry completeness as a percentage of covered sites.
Cross-reference the telemetry gap with near-term event pressure.
Translate the gap into a service-risk statement for field execution.
inferenceConstraints: Do not call the fryer dataset complete when fryer_count telemetry is still empty.
Do not ignore event timing while evaluating the telemetry gap.
Do not provide service guidance without stating the precision limit.
outputRequirements: State coverage and telemetry completeness separately.
Provide the posture until telemetry is complete.

sourceRecords.recordHint: fryer-row coverage and populated fryer_count totals
near-term event window used to evaluate telemetry urgency

notes: Coverage completeness and telemetry completeness are intentionally treated as different states.
This card preserves the current precision limit instead of masking it.
